sources.title: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
sources.usedFor: Hierarchy, sidebar discipline, and header/chrome behavior definitions.
borrowed: Treating sidebar, tabs, and headers as one behavior-defined shell family.
Keeping density high without turning the shell into a dashboard wall.
rejected: Replicating Linear's product-specific chrome or brand hierarchy directly.

sources.title: Custom Views – Linear Docs
borrowed: Keeping saved views explicitly owned and discoverable from the sidebar.
Treating saved views as repeatable working modes rather than temporary filters.
rejected: Team-specific or workspace-specific taxonomy that does not fit the hub desk shell.

sources.title: Data table – Carbon Design System
sources.usedFor: Dense row sizing, toolbar restraint, and row-height consistency.
borrowed: Consistent row height and toolbar restraint for dense operational rows.
Avoiding pseudo-spreadsheet behavior when the interaction model is more complex than a table.
rejected: Letting the whole shell collapse into a data-table-first experience before 327 owns the workbench.

sources.title: Content guide – NHS digital service manual
sources.usedFor: Plain transactional wording and calm start-state copy.
borrowed: Short, explicit, service-style wording for saved views, status signals, and route hosts.

sources.title: Disclosure (Show/Hide) Pattern – WAI-ARIA APG
sources.usedFor: Reviewing disclosure semantics for secondary route-host reveals.
borrowed: Use button semantics for any reveal control and keep expanded state explicit.
rejected: Adding multiple persistent disclosure stacks to the start-of-day surface.

sources.title: Understanding SC 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
sources.usedFor: Focus visibility with sticky masthead and status-strip content.
borrowed: Using root scroll padding so sticky shell chrome does not hide focused elements.
rejected: Accepting partially obscured focus as a default shell posture.

sources.title: Best Practices – Playwright
sources.usedFor: User-visible locators and resilient browser proof.
borrowed: Testing user-visible behavior instead of implementation details.
rejected: CSS-class-only locators for shell continuity checks.

sources.title: Isolation – Playwright
borrowed: One isolated browser context per proof scenario.

sources.title: Snapshot testing – Playwright
borrowed: Using ARIA snapshots for broad structural checks and targeted assertions for precise semantics.
rejected: Oversized snapshots that hide specific regressions.

sources.title: Visual comparisons – Playwright
borrowed: Deterministic visual capture with committed screenshot outputs.
rejected: Loose visual checks without explicit baselines or trace capture.

sources.title: Web Vitals – web.dev
borrowed: Treating 75th percentile desktop and mobile performance as the right shell benchmark.
rejected: Metric-heavy hero dashboards in the start-of-day view.
